scenario.title: Headless agent-browser real-Chromium acceptance and evidence foundation
preconditions: The supervisor proves the previous lease terminated, acquires the sole migration-wide lease, and supplies the clean-head Gooseweb URL; the reviewer never starts or configures the stack.
The reviewer creates a globally unique named agent-browser session with explicit --headed false, --engine chrome, and the recorded user-installed Chrome executable path.
Project, user, and environment headed settings are rejected before attachment; no alternate browser framework, persistent prior context, fixture query, or browser-side authority is used.

The reviewer records binary/version identity, browser process arguments, HeadlessChrome user-agent evidence, candidate/served head and tree, lease tuple, and fresh-context nonce before the first action.
The source-controlled procedure and p03 evidence descriptor validate before any browser result can be considered.

Complete unfiltered network evidence is retained as initial-load, ordinary-reload, agent-browser no-cache-header hard-reload, and second-fresh-context segments in that exact order; every raw request is attributed to one segment.

The hard reload uses agent-browser set headers with Cache-Control: no-cache and Pragma: no-cache, ordinary agent-browser reload, observed request-header/navigation/reconstruction evidence, and immediate agent-browser set headers {} cleanup.

expected_runtime: one deterministic completed turn record with the exact submitted text
one terminal event with the deterministic source text and no duplicate ID
expected_goosetower: the P02 observer records the materialized session and served frame or explicitly records unavailable evidence without inference
the supervisor-provided clean head, source configuration, and lease tuple remain unchanged throughout the attempt
the first divergent layer is named when any product baseline prevents terminal convergence
expected_worker_store: the active production Worker entry is app/realtime/worker/realtime-command-worker.ts and consumes only served gateway frames
the redacted P02 observer captures bounded Worker/store state without inferring unavailable data
a stale or reused context nonce fails before visible-state evidence is accepted
expected_visible: the deterministic action is submitted once through Agent thread composer and Send agent thread message
ordinary reload, hard reload, navigation away/back, reconnect, and fresh context preserve or honestly expose the mapped product baseline

expected_runtime: one deterministic team message and intended delivery record
one matching team_message.created event with monotonic source order
expected_goosetower: the real team materializer contains the deterministic message and its delivery
one matching team_workspace patch/frame preserves the stable message identity
expected_worker_store: the selected team workspace contains the deterministic message exactly once
the delivery remains linked to the same message ID
expected_visible: the deterministic broadcast action is visible once in Team Comms
the injected delivery state is visible without a phantom success

required: P02 fixed session, turn, message, delivery, clock, IDs, epoch, and source sequences
delivery records for intended recipients only
bounded replay plus duplicate, gap, epoch, disconnect, delay, rejection, and terminal facts
unknown public fields retained and every observer value redacted at capture
real runtime client, SSE fan-in, materializer, and gateway path consume the P02 public source
materialized state and decoded frames carry stable identity and explicit source/gateway cursors
the supervisor owns all observer listener configuration and exposes only bounded capture-time-redacted evidence
the active production decoder and realtime-command-worker path consume deterministic captured frames
read-only observer reports exact normalized state without inferring unavailable evidence
the explicit debug-frames observer is absent from production/default builds and never becomes browser authority
exact deterministic turn and team text visible once through semantic controls

golden_goose_acceptance_lessons: no composer without a selected thread/team
live projection and window coverage honesty

gooselake.reason: P03 observes P02 deterministic source records only; it changes no Gooselake behavior or product truth.

goosetower.reason: P03 records the supervisor-provided Tower materialization/frame evidence; it changes no gateway or materializer behavior.

gooseweb.reason: P03 adds source-controlled browser procedure, evidence descriptor validation, and self-tests only; route, Worker, store, React, and stack lifecycle behavior are unchanged.

auth_account_state.reason: No auth/account state changes; cookies, CSRF values, bearer tokens, tickets, query values, and provider auth are prohibited and redacted at capture.

protocol_generated_code.reason: No Protobuf schema or generated output is edited; WebSocket evidence uses P02 observers or explicitly unavailable redacted evidence.

api_narrative_docs.reason: No product HTTP behavior, route, OpenAPI artifact, or narrative API behavior changes.

assertions: composer and primary send action are inside viewport
focus, Escape, and return behavior are correct where a sheet is used

empty.expectation: Clear, non-fixture empty state and no stale rows.

loading.expectation: Honest loading state without fake message text.

stale_gap_replaying.expectation: Visible stale/replay state; no command is presented as converged.

rejection_conflict.expectation: Specific terminal rejection and no phantom visible success.

permission_denied.reason: P03 exercises the existing owner/dev-auth flow; RBAC denial becomes product scope in P15-P17.

unsupported_provider.reason: The deterministic source-backed P03 procedure is provider-independent.

terminal_error.expectation: Specific failure remains visible and cannot be classified as success.

default_fixture_leak_assertions: URL has no gooseweb fixture parameter.
Visible text contains no dev fixture markers or fake entities.
Fixture-only globals and controls are absent.
fixtures.gating: Any visual fixture must require both import.meta.env.DEV and an explicit query parameter.

baseline_detected.description: Runtime message/delivery/event and injection succeed while Team Comms remains empty.
Runtime terminal truth succeeds while the materialized/browser session remains turn_running.
The semantic Start lead control produces no runtime/materialized session, pending state, or visible terminal rejection, so the source-backed Team Comms precondition cannot be established.

baseline_detected.first_divergent_layer: Gooseweb Worker/store to React connection-state projection
baseline_detected.description: Offline/online and fresh-context reconstruction do not expose an honest connection state; the top level reports degraded while source health reports live.
After reload at 520x900, Team Comms is absent from exposed semantic controls, so the critical surface is unreachable.
P01 and agent-browser provide no redacted WebSocket frame observer, so frame behavior is recorded as unavailable and never inferred.
Every fresh browser attempt requests GET /favicon.ico and receives HTTP 404; the failed request remains blocked until the static asset route is corrected.

A deterministic source-sequence gap reaches real RuntimeSseFanIn, whose Live to GapDetected transition is rejected by the current source-health lifecycle algebra in debug builds. P02 detects and localizes the baseline without changing P06 product behavior.

baseline_detected.first_divergent_layer: Goosetower runtime client total request deadline → RuntimeSseFanIn cursor continuity
baseline_detected.description: The production GooselakeRuntimeClient applies its 30-second total request deadline to the infinite SSE response. Reqwest returns a timeout-driven body decode transport error despite 10-second keepalives; RuntimeSseFanIn reconnects from cursor 1 after already parsing/deduplicating rows 2 and 3. P02 precisely observes this P06 product defect without changing or overriding production timeout/cursor semantics.

baseline_detected.first_divergent_layer: Goosetower session-detail snapshot kind to Gooseweb decoder/store
baseline_detected.description: In a fresh epoch-002 stack, reset row 1 and its terminal assistant text traverse the real live path and render correctly, but ordinary reload reconstructs the same session as No messages yet because the session-detail snapshot kind is decoded/stored without terminal detail. P02 records the exact pre-existing reconstruction defect from attempt 5 without correcting or approving P08 product scope.

schema_migration.reason: P03 adds versioned verification procedure and evidence contracts only; no persistent product schema changes.

backfill.reason: No product data changes or historical transformation occur.

compatibility.reason: The P03 validator is additive and preserves the P01/P02 manifest schema, documents, registry hashes, and evidence schemas.

rollback.reason: Verification artifacts are removed by reverting the P03 commit; product state is untouched.

performance.reason: P05 owns product budgets; P03 measures only procedure overhead and evidence completeness.

security.reason: P03 rejects unredacted bearer, ticket, credential, cookie, CSRF, query-value, provider-auth, secret-config, and raw-image evidence at capture.

data_retention_gc.reason: P03 uses a fresh ephemeral Chromium context and retains only redacted review evidence; it creates no durable product objects.

deployment.reason: No production deployment, browser package, binary, cache, config, CI browser runner, or stack lifecycle change is introduced.

provenance_reuse.reason: Golden Goose AgentRuntimePanel, MainWindow, and LeftSideBar tests were read as semantic-control and reconstruction references; no source code was copied or adapted.

native_tauri_assumptions_removed.reason: No Golden Goose implementation code was reused.
